comments.body: You can see this answer: stackoverflow.com/a/38415544/5961908 This should help.
That was before everybody and their aunt implemented night light. Certainly there must be an intent or something to handle this built-in feature?

answers.body:
There's no official public API in Android to enable/disable Night Light (Blue Light Filter).

The Stack Overflow posts you found discuss dark theme toggling, but Night Light operates at a system level. Android restricts this to prevent apps from hijacking display settings.

Workaround (user-facing only)

You can guide users to enable it manually by opening the relevant settings screen:

// Opens Android's Night Light settings (API 27+)
Intent intent = new Intent(android.provider.Settings.ACTION_NIGHT_DISPLAY_SETTINGS);
intent.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK);
context.startActivity(intent);

Notes

  • Samsung/other OEMs might have proprietary APIs (e.g., Samsung's Blue Light Filter), but these are device-specific and often require OEM SDKs with limited access.
  • No reliable cross-device solution exists. Google restricts this intentionally for user control.

If your app requires this functionality, you'll need to document the manual setup steps clearly to direct users to settings. Avoid claiming "one-tap enable" in your app. It's impossible without system privileges.

title: How to enable NightShift/Night Light/Blue Light Filter programmatically in Android?
body:
I am trying to enable Blue Light Filter programmatically in Android. Is there any API or function in android that can help me enable / disable / schedule Blue Light Filter option?

For clarification, the feature is called Night Shift in iPhones, Night Light in Google Pixels and Blue Light Filter in Samsung Galaxy. Also there maybe other names. It reduces blue light emitted from screen so that it is less stressful for eyes to look at the screen and helps to sleep.

So far, I have searching on developer.android.com, and I have come across the following:

https://developer.android.com/reference/android/app/UiModeManager

However, when I tried using it on my device which is running on Android 10, and nothing changed. I have found following Questions on Stackoverflow on the API:

To implement Android night mode, using UiModeManager and enable car mode, but show a notification, it may not the good way

Android Night Mode without CarModeEnabled

Android Day/Night Mode. UiModeManafer.getNightMode

How to enable night mode programmatically?

But it discusses about the Night Mode which is actually enabling / disabling dark theme. But what I need is to enable, disable the Blue Light Filter or Night Light, mainly which reduces blue light emitted from the screen. Is there any available API that will allow me to change it?
